description: Iraq invaded and occupied Kuwait, creating an immediate threat to Gulf oil supply and shipping security.

event_name: Asian Financial Crisis begins in Thailand
description: Thailand dropped the baht peg, triggering a regional financial crisis and weaker expected energy demand.
Terrorist attacks in the United States increased uncertainty and disrupted air travel and economic activity.
Military operations in Iraq raised concerns about production and transport risks in a major oil-producing region.
source_name: U.S. Army Center of Military History

event_name: Hurricane Katrina makes Gulf Coast landfall
description: Katrina damaged Gulf Coast infrastructure and interrupted offshore production, refining, and transport.
source_name: NOAA Office of Response and Restoration

description: The bankruptcy intensified the global financial crisis and expectations of a sharp contraction in oil demand.
Escalating violence in Libya raised concern over exports from an important light-sweet crude producer.

event_name: EU embargo on Iranian oil fully enters into force
description: The European Union oil embargo restricted Iranian exports and tightened expected supply availability.

event_name: OPEC maintains 30 million b/d production ceiling
description: OPEC chose not to reduce its production ceiling despite growing supply, reinforcing expectations of oversupply.
OPEC members agreed to a coordinated production adjustment intended to accelerate market rebalancing.

event_name: United States announces withdrawal from JCPOA
description: The decision began the reimposition of U.S. sanctions related to Iran, increasing concern about future exports.

event_name: Attack on Abqaiq and Khurais facilities
description: Attacks temporarily disrupted major Saudi crude processing and production capacity.

event_name: WHO characterizes COVID-19 as a pandemic
description: Travel restrictions and reduced economic activity caused an exceptional collapse in expected oil demand.
Participating producers agreed to reduce crude production by 9.7 million barrels per day from May 2020.
Storage constraints and collapsing demand pushed WTI futures negative; Brent fell to a multi-decade low the next day.

event_name: OPEC+ approves gradual monthly production increases
description: OPEC+ approved a plan to increase overall production by 0.4 million barrels per day each month.

event_name: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine
description: The invasion intensified supply-security concerns involving one of the world’s largest oil exporters.

event_name: United States bans imports of Russian energy
description: The United States prohibited imports of Russian oil, liquefied natural gas, and coal.

event_name: OPEC+ announces 2 million b/d production cut
description: OPEC+ agreed to lower overall production by 2 million barrels per day from November 2022.
